poem.content:
Upon a stool, a tourist sits to see,
Tomorrow's landscape through a tinted lens,
A local market, where bright colors spree,
And various blooms their petals now dispense.

An apple gleams, a polished, scarlet prize,
Beside a flower, frail and pale and white,
A hobby stall, bathed in the morning skies,
Where shadows blur, obscuring day from night.

He watched the change, a subtle, creeping thing,
As faces scatter, voices rise and fall,
A supreme artist, time, on tireless wing,
Adds texture to the memories of all.

A start is made, a sketch upon the page,
The margin filled with details, small and deep,
Escaping limits of a gilded cage,
While in the heart, a solemn promise keep.

The world unfolds, a canvas wide and free,
And fate and chance will write the final mark,
Of every soul for all eternity.
